title: commelina diffusa subsp. aquatica
intro: Commelina diffusa subsp. aquatica, also known as wandering jew or water spangles, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Commelinaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Commelina diffusa subsp. aquatica is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can also be used topically in the form of creams or ointments.
